figures.caption: Fig. 1. The overall architecture of the NEMESIS backbone. The input superpatch is divided into volume patches and processed through the patch embedding module. The framework utilizes dual-masking and MATB-based encoders and decoders to learn high-level anatomical representations in the latent space.
Fig. 3. Detailed architecture of the 3D Adaptive Patch Embedding module, integrating Linear Projection (LP) and Superpatch-Embedder (SE) pathways.
